可

- ❼ This exam has 3 problems worth a total of 100 points.
- ❼ The packet contains 1 blank page, which is page 10. If you use a blank page to solve a problem, make a note in the original box and clearly label which problem you are solving on the blank page.
- ❼ DO NOT REMOVE THE STAPLE OR SEPARATE THE PAGES OF YOUR EXAM. DOING SO WILL RESULT IN A -10 POINT PENALTY!
- ❼ You may have pencils, eraser, pen, tissues, water, and your RPI ID card on your desk. Place everything else on the floor under your chair. Electronic equipment, including computers, cell phones, calculators, music players, smart watches, cameras, etc. is not permitted and must be turned 'off' (not just vibrate).
- ❼ Raise your hand if you need to ask a proctor something that is NOT related to one of the questions on the test.
- ❼ Please state clearly any assumptions that you made in interpreting a question. Unless otherwise stated you may use any technique that we have discussed in lecture, lab, or on the homework.
- ❼ Please write neatly. If we can't read your solution, we can't give you full credit for your work.
- ❼ You do not need to write #include statements for STL libraries. Writing std:: is optional. The keyword auto is not allowed.

## Problem 1: Multiple-choice questions. There is only one correct answer to each multiple choice question. [ /36]

1. (3 points) Assume ptr points to the beginning of an integer array. If the size of an int data type in memory is 4 bytes, what does the following statement do?
2. (A) Moves ptr by 3 bytes forward.
3. (B) Moves ptr by 3 elements forward.
4. (C) Moves ptr to the last element of the array.
5. (D) Causes a segmentation fault.
2. (3 points) The following function aims to insert a node at the end of a linked list, but there is one line missing, how should we add the missing line so this function correctly inserts a node at the end of the linked list?
7. void insertEnd(Node* head, int data) { Node* newNode = new Node(data); while (head-&gt;next != nullptr) { head = head-&gt;next; } // Missing line here } (A) head-&gt; next = nullptr; (B) newNode-&gt; next = head; (C) head-&gt; next = newNode; (D) delete newNode;
3. (3 points) What happens if a function returns a local pointer like this?
9. (A) The function correctly returns a pointer to the array.
10. (B) Memory is dynamically allocated, so it is safe.
11. (C) The function returns a dangling pointer which points to a memory location that has already been deallocated, and this would lead to undefined behavior.
12. (D) The compiler prevents this and throws an error.
4. (3 points) What is the output of this program?

```
ptr = ptr + 3;
```

```
int* createArray() { int arr[5] = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}; return arr; }
```

```
#include <iostream> #include <vector> using namespace std; int main() { vector<int> v = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}; for (size_t i = 0; i < v.size(); i++) { v.push_back(v[i] + 10); if (v.size() > 7) break; } for (int x : v) cout << x << " "; return 0; } (A) 1 2 3 4 5 11 12 13 14
```

```
(B) 1 2 3 4 5 11 12 13 (C) 1 2 3 4 5 11 12 (D) 1 2 3 4 5 11 (E) Infinite loop
```

5. (3 points)
6. (3 points)

```
What will be the output of this program? #include <iostream> #include <string> using namespace std; void mystery(string& str) { int i = 0, j = str.length() -1; while (i < j) { swap(str[i++], str[j--]); // as its name suggests, // this function just swaps str[i++] and str[j--] } } int main() { string s = "ABCDE"; mystery(s); cout << s; return 0; } (A) EDCBA (B) ABCDE (C) ABEDC (D) ACBDE (E) ACBED (F) BCDEA (G) CDEBA What will be the output of this program? #include <iostream> using namespace std; void mystery(string& str, int i) { if (i >= str.size()) return; mystery(str, i + 1); cout << str[i]; } int main() { string s = "hello"; mystery(s, 0); return 0; } (A) hello (B) olleh (C) hlelo (D) ohell (E) hell (F) o
```

```
(G) ell
```

- (H) llo
7. (3 points)
8. (3 points)

```
What will be the output of this program? #include <iostream> using namespace std; class Node { public: int data; Node* next; Node(int val) : data(val), next(nullptr) {} }; void mystery(Node* head) { if (!head || !head->next) return; mystery(head->next->next); cout << head->data << " "; } int main() { Node* head = new Node(1); head->next = new Node(2); head->next->next = new Node(3); head->next->next->next = new Node(4); head->next->next->next->next = new Node(5); mystery(head); return 0; } (A) 1 3 5 (B) 5 3 1 (C) 1 2 3 4 5 (D) 5 4 3 2 1 (E) 1 2 3 (F) 3 2 1 (G) 1 3 (H) 3 1 (I) 1 2 4 (J) 4 2 1 What will be the output of this program? #include <iostream> using namespace std; class Node { public: int data; Node* next; Node(int val) : data(val), next(nullptr) {} }; void modify(Node* head) { while (head && head->next) {
```

Name:

- 9.

```
head->data += head->next->data; head = head->next->next; } } void printList(Node* head) { while (head) { cout << head->data << " "; head = head->next; } } int main() { Node* head = new Node(1); head->next = new Node(2); head->next->next = new Node(3); head->next->next->next = new Node(4); head->next->next->next->next = new Node(5); modify(head); printList(head); return 0; } (A) 1 2 3 4 5 (B) 3 2 7 4 5 (C) 3 2 3 4 5 (D) 1 2 7 4 5 (E) 1 5 3 9 5 (F) 3 2 7 4 (G) 3 2 7 (3 points) What will be the output of this program? #include <iostream> using namespace std; class Node { public: int data; Node* next; Node(int d) : data(d), next(nullptr) {} }; void mystery(Node*& head) { if (!head) return; Node* temp = head; head = head->next; delete temp; } int main() { Node* head = new Node(10); head->next = new Node(20); head->next->next = new Node(30); head->next->next->next = new Node(40); mystery(head); cout << head->data << endl; }
```

- (A) 10
- (B) 20
- (C) 30
- (D) 40
- (E) Segmentation fault
- (F) Compiler error
10. (3 points) Consider the following program, when will the copy constructor be called?
- (A) When a is created.
- (B) When a is passed to func by value.
- (C) After func finishes execution.
- (D) The copy constructor is never called in this program.
11. (3 points) Consider the following program, how many times is the assignment operator called in this program?
- (D) 3

```
#include <iostream> class MyClass { public: MyClass() { std::cout << "Constructor Called\n"; } MyClass(const MyClass &other) { std::cout << "Copy Constructor Called\n"; } ~MyClass() { std::cout << "Destructor Called\n"; } }; void func(MyClass obj) { std::cout << "Inside func\n"; } int main() { MyClass a; func(a); return 0; }
```

```
#include <iostream> class MyClass { public: MyClass() { std::cout << "Constructor Called\n"; } MyClass(const MyClass &other) { std::cout << "Copy Constructor Called\n"; } MyClass& operator=(const MyClass &other) { std::cout << "Assignment Operator Called\n"; return *this; } ~MyClass() { std::cout << "Destructor Called\n"; } }; int main() { MyClass obj1; MyClass obj2 = obj1; MyClass obj3(obj2); } (A) 0 (B) 1 (C) 2
```

Name:

12. (3 points) What will be the output of this program?
2. (A) Constructor Called. Copy Constructor Called. Assignment Operator Called. Destructor Called.
3. (B) Constructor Called. Copy Constructor Called. Assignment Operator Called. Destructor Called.
4. (C) Constructor Called. Assignment Operator Called. Destructor Called.
5. (D) Constructor Called. Copy Constructor Called. Destructor Called.
6. (E) Constructor Called. Constructor Called. Assignment Operator Called. Destructor Called. Destructor Called.

```
#include <iostream> class MyClass { public: MyClass(int x) : data(x) { std::cout << "Constructor Called. "; } MyClass(const MyClass &other) : data(other.data) { std::cout << "Copy Constructor Called. "; } MyClass& operator=(const MyClass &other) { data = other.data; std::cout << "Assignment Operator Called. "; return *this; } ~MyClass() { std::cout << "Destructor Called. "; } private: int data; }; int main() { MyClass a(10); MyClass b(20); b = a; }
```

Name:

Problem 2: STL Lists [

/32]

Write a templated recursive function in C++ that calculates the sum of elements in an std::list &lt; T &gt; .

Constraints:

1. The function should work for any numeric type (int, double, float, etc.). 2. It must be implemented recursively.

3. The function should take a const std::list &lt; T &gt; by reference as its only parameter. 4. Do not modify this list.

// write your answer here:

Name:

Name:

Problem 3: Merge Linked Lists . [ /32] Write a templated recursive function in C++ that merges two sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list. Assume that the linked list is implemented with the following Node class:

```
template <class T> class Node { public: T data; Node* next; Node(T val) : data(val), next(nullptr) {} };
```

Constraints: 1. Your function should work for any type (int, double, float, or any custom type). 2. It must be implemented recursively. 3. Your function should take two pointers to the heads of the two linked lists, and return a pointer to the merged sorted linked list. 4. Do not call new to allocate memory.
